note: A MODALITY, not a power-system: a cross-cutting HOW that many traditions can be expressed through. Where the Reach crafts are WHAT you can do (Vivimancy, Enginecraft...), a modality is the CHANNEL by which will becomes effect. This one — Inscription — is will made structure: a pattern (rune, sigil, glyph-string) that DOES what its structure MEANS, read and executed by the substrate (nanites, the lattice, the Precursor mesh) that saturates the valley. Inspired by Death Gate rune-magic (structure-as-spell) married to the nanite substrate. The same ability can be cast fast-and-fragile by voice/gesture, or slow-and-durable by inscription. Authored by Aevi (PO), from Erik's Death Gate + nanite framing.
principle: Will can be expressed many ways. Speaking it is fast and fades. Inscribing it is slow and holds. The nanite/Precursor substrate that pervades the valley is a reader of structure — it enacts patterns it can parse. A rune is not a symbol OF power; it is an INSTRUCTION the substrate runs. Complexity of the inscription = complexity of the effect; precision of the structure = precision of the result; a malformed rune runs malformed. This is why the Precursor deep is dangerous: it is dense with substrate that will read and run almost anything, correctly or not.

channels.name: Spoken / Gestured Will
channels.description: The default cast — will shaped by voice, gesture, focus. Fast, flexible, gone when you stop. How most abilities resolve by default.
channels.tradeoff: No lingering effect; interrupt-able; the substrate reads a fleeting instruction and stops when you do.

channels.name: Inscription (rune-work)
channels.speed: slow (a scene, a rest, a ritual)
channels.durability: persistent — holds until erased, worn, or overwritten
channels.description: Will made structure — a rune drawn, carved, woven, or traced in nanite-ink that the substrate reads and KEEPS running. A ward that stands for days; a healing sigil that works while worn; a trap that waits; a door-glyph that answers only you.
channels.tradeoff: Slow to make; fixed once set (a rune does one structured thing); a flawed inscription runs its flaw; a clever enemy can read or deface it.
channels.enables: Persistent effects, delayed effects, effects that work without the caster present, effects layered into objects and places. This is how you leave power BEHIND you.
channels.gmGuidance: Let any ability be inscribed rather than cast for a time+durability tradeoff. A Vivimancer can INSCRIBE Quickening into a field to bloom for a season; an Enginewright inscribes a command that runs when they're gone; a rune-caster inscribes a reading-ward that flags what crosses it. The rune's STRUCTURE encodes the ability + its parameters.

channels.name: Attuned / Willed-Direct
channels.description: Will expressed with no external channel at all — direct instruction to the substrate by trained intent (the Numen road, deep attunement). No word, no mark; the substrate reads the will itself.
channels.tradeoff: Costliest and hardest; only where substrate is dense and the wielder deeply attuned; holds only while concentrated.
channels.enables: The subtlest, fastest work — and the most personal; the substrate reads YOU, which means it can misread you.

nanite_substrate.what: GM-EYES-ONLY: the 'magic' of the valley is substrate — Precursor nanite-mesh and lattice, denser near the deep sites, thinner in the safe margins. It reads structured will and enacts it. Abilities work BECAUSE the substrate runs them; the poles/axes describe what KINDS of instruction the local substrate favors.
nanite_substrate.implication_purity: This ties to the coordinate model: a Reach's dominant pole is the KIND of instruction its substrate runs best. Near a pole, the substrate is dense and eager — more power, and it runs your malformed will too. At the origin, substrate is thin — little works, little goes wrong.
nanite_substrate.implication_runes: Runes and inscription work best where substrate is dense enough to read them. A rune drawn in the Salt Meadows barely hums; the same rune in the Gearlands roars — and may attract attention.
nanite_substrate.glimpsed_never_explained: The player should feel this as 'magic that behaves like it's being READ by something,' never get a tech-manual. What the substrate IS, and whose it was, stays the deep question.

buildImplication: Inscription becomes a cross-tradition option: abilities gain an optional 'inscribable' flag + a rune-cost. A new light content layer of RUNE definitions (structure → effect) can be authored. Character origins can include a native channel-affinity (a born inscriber, a born direct-willer). Ties the existing Rune Dice (reads pole-structure) to rune-CASTING (writes pole-structure) — same glyph-language, read and write.
